incident_details: On November 11, 2021, the Maryland Office of the Attorney General reported a data breach involving Derby Industries, LLC, which first learned of the incident on September 4, 2021, due to a malware attack that lasted until September 8, 2021. The Maine Office of the Attorney General reported a data breach involving Derby Industries, LLC on November 12, 2021. The breach occurred between August 26, 2021, and September 8, 2021, as a result of external hacking, potentially affecting 2,847 individuals, including 1 Maine resident. The compromised information includes personal data such as names, bank account numbers, and Social Security numbers, and identity theft protection services were offered.

incident_details: The Maryland Office of the Attorney General reported that Intuit Inc. experienced unauthorized access to customer information between January 16, 2017 and April 17, 2017, affecting 177 Maryland customers of the TurboTax service. incident_details: On June 7, 2013, the New Hampshire Department of Justice reported that Polaris Industries Inc. experienced a security deficiency that allowed dealer applications submitted between April 1, 2007, and May 8, 2013, to be accessible online. Polaris took immediate action to rectify the situation and disabled the dealer application site.

incident_details: UNC5537 (ShinyHunters) conducted large-scale credential stuffing campaign using stolen credentials from infostealer malware. Targeted 165+ Snowflake customer accounts lacking MFA. Used custom tools including FROSTBITE/rapeflake for automated data exfiltration. Major victims include AT&T, Ticketmaster, Santander Bank.
information_affected: Customer databases containing PII, financial records, call/text metadata, payment information

incident_details: The Wisconsin Department of Justice reported a data breach involving Volkswagen Group of America, Inc. on June 11, 2021. The breach occurred between August 2019 and May 2021, affecting approximately 3.3 million individuals, including 25,738 Wisconsin consumers whose contact and vehicle information was impacted. Sensitive information included driver's license numbers for 1,024 Wisconsin consumers, with other compromised data such as dates of birth and Social Security numbers being very limited.

incident_details: The New Hampshire Department of Justice reported a data breach involving Wabtec Corporation on December 30, 2022. The breach, discovered on June 26, 2022, was caused by malware introduced as early as March 15, 2022, affecting the personal information of three New Hampshire residents, specifically their names and Social Security numbers.

The Maryland Office of the Attorney General reported that Wabtec Corporation experienced a data breach involving malware encryption on June 26, 2022, affecting 1,774 Maryland residents. The compromised information includes names, Social Security numbers, financial account information, payment card numbers, driver's licenses/state ID numbers, passport numbers, medical treatment/diagnosis information, health insurance information, and/or account usernames and passwords.

incident_details: The California Office of the Attorney General reported a data breach involving Yahoo! Inc. on September 22, 2016. incident_details: On April 7, 2023, the Washington State Office of the Attorney General reported a data breach incident involving Yum! Brands, Inc., which occurred on January 13, 2023, due to a ransomware cyberattack. Approximately 1,220 individuals were affected, with exposed personal information including names, Social Security numbers, driver's license numbers, and medical information.

incident_details: The Oregon Department of Justice reported that Yum! Brands, Inc. experienced a data breach affecting approximately 300,000 individuals. The breach occurred between January 13, 2023, and January 14, 2023, with the incident discovered on March 9, 2023, and notification sent on April 14, 2023.

incident_details: UNC6395 threat actors compromised Salesloft Drift OAuth tokens as part of supply chain attack affecting 700+ organizations. Gained unauthorized access to Zscaler's Salesforce instance exposing customer support information and business contact data.
information_affected: Customer contact information, support case content

incident_details: Zscaler experienced a breach where an attacker gained unauthorized access to a limited number of internal systems. No customer data was affected.
information_affected: Internal systems
